extra.reasoning:
This is a state machine. The property G(opted_out implies not sent) requires sent=false in every reachable state where opted_out=true. The transition t_resume_glitch breaks this: on send_attempt with guard (opted_out and ticks>=8), it sets sent:=true while opted_out remains true.

Trace of the witness (10 events, within the 12-event bound):
- 8 tick events drive ticks from 0 up to its cap of 8 (t_tick increments until 8, also setting sent:=false each time).
- optout (t_optout) sets opted_out:=true, sent:=false; ticks stays at 8.
- send_attempt: now opted_out=true and ticks=8>=8, so t_resume_glitch is enabled and fires, setting sent:=true.

Resulting state: opted_out=true, sent=true, ticks=8 — directly violating opted_out implies not sent.

Note on rule interaction/priority: on this final send_attempt both t_send_blocked (guard opted_out) and t_resume_glitch (guard opted_out and ticks>=8) are enabled. Since the property must hold on EVERY reachable execution and at least one enabled execution (firing t_resume_glitch, the last-listed transition) reaches the violating state, the property is violated. The glitch is only reachable once ticks has been driven to the 8 cap, which the eight leading tick events accomplish before opting out.
